title: Was Antigravity Discovered In The FIFTIES?
summary: This interview segment discusses the 'Golden Age of general relativity' and a mysterious surge of interest in anti-gravity research during the 1950s by aerospace companies and top physicists. The discussion centers on whether this research quietly failed or succeeded and became classified.

events.description: A series of articles by Talbert published in the Miami Herald and other newspapers revealed that aerospace engineering companies and top physicists were receiving grants to research anti-gravity

key_claims.claim: There was a bizarre surge of activity in general relativity research between Einstein's time and the 1950s after a period of limited development
Multiple aerospace engineering companies became suddenly interested in anti-gravity research in the 1950s
Top-level physicists including DeWitt were receiving grants to study anti-gravity
The anti-gravity research either failed and was abandoned, or succeeded and went classified (black)
